data_coverage_summary: Tag Data Events publish RFID read telemetry including tag identifiers, antenna context, signal strength, phase, channel, and access-result metadata.
buyer_guidance: Use Tag Data Events when the integration needs high-fidelity RFID observation data for downstream automation, visibility, or correlation against an external asset model.
overview: Tag Data Events are a pure telemetry surface for RFID observations. The schema includes detailed read metadata such as EPC, antenna, RSSI, phase, channel, and read count, but it does not define a reader asset catalog or any writeback mechanics. Asset data is Not Supported. Telemetry data is Supported. Writeback is Not Supported.

available_data.description: The event model is centered on low-level RFID tag observations and their signal characteristics.

ingest_considerations: This schema describes observation events, so any physical asset modeling should be handled by correlating the tag data to an external asset registry.
Because the payloads are read-only event data, writeback should be modeled through a separate Zebra control surface if needed.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Classification is based on the published tag-data event schema covering EPC, antenna, RSSI, phase, channel, access-result, and read-count fields, with no documented asset CRUD or mutation semantics.
